title: Best VPN for Torrenting in 2026: Fast, Safe, No-Log Options
description: Which VPN actually works for torrenting in 2026? We compare speeds, P2P policies, kill switches, and privacy records to find the best options.
content:
## Why Torrenting Needs a Different VPN

Not every VPN works for torrenting. Some block P2P traffic entirely. Others allow it but throttle speeds or log connection metadata. The VPNs that work well for torrenting have P2P-optimized servers, verified no-log policies, a kill switch that actually works, and speeds that do not make large file downloads painful.

## Top Picks for 2026

**Mullvad** is the privacy benchmark. No account required, payment by cash or crypto accepted, open-source clients. Speeds are fast enough for most torrenting. The main downside is no port forwarding, which slows seeding ratios on private trackers.

**ProtonVPN** (Paid tier) has the best combination of verified privacy and torrenting performance. Their no-log policy has been independently audited. P2P is allowed on servers in most countries. Port forwarding is available. Swiss jurisdiction.

**NordVPN** (with reservations) has fast P2P-optimized servers and a large network. For pure download speed on public torrents it performs well. For high-stakes privacy, the track record is weaker than Mullvad or Proton.

## Kill Switch: Non-Negotiable

A kill switch cuts your internet connection if the VPN drops. Without one, your real IP is exposed the moment the VPN disconnects. Test it before you rely on it.

## Recommendation

For most users: ProtonVPN paid. Strong audited privacy, fast speeds, port forwarding, and a track record that holds up. For anonymity above all else: Mullvad.
